---
title: Settings & fields
description: Categories, field kinds, conditions and expanding sections.
---

The `settings.categories` tree is what users see when they configure an instance. Categories group
fields; fields map to environment variables on the container.

## Categories

```json
{
  "id": "world",
  "label": "World",
  "description": "World generation and gameplay",
  "children": [/* fields or nested categories */]
}
```

Categories nest: `children` accepts both fields and further categories, so you can build
sections and subsections.

## Fields

Every field connects a UI control to an environment variable:

```json
{
  "key": "MAX_PLAYERS",
  "label": "Max players",
  "description": "How many players can join at once.",
  "kind": "NUMBER",
  "defaultValue": 20,
  "min": 1,
  "max": 200
}
```

<TypeTable
  type={{
    key: {
      description: "The environment variable this field sets on the container.",
      type: "string",
      required: true,
    },
    label: {
      description: "Label shown in the form.",
      type: "string",
      required: true,
    },
    description: {
      description: "Help text under the field. May be null.",
      type: "string | null",
      required: true,
    },
    kind: {
      description: "Which control to render; see the table below.",
      type: "FieldKind",
      required: true,
    },
    defaultValue: {
      description: "Pre-filled value.",
      type: "any",
    },
    placeholder: {
      description: "Placeholder text for empty inputs.",
      type: "string",
    },
    options: {
      description:
        "Choices for SELECT / COMBOBOX kinds: { value, label, expand? }.",
      type: "Option[]",
    },
    min: { description: "Minimum for numeric kinds.", type: "number" },
    max: { description: "Maximum for numeric kinds.", type: "number" },
    step: {
      description: "Step for sliders/numbers (must be > 0).",
      type: "number",
    },
    unit: { description: 'Unit suffix, e.g. "MB".', type: "string" },
    required: {
      description: "Whether the field must be filled.",
      type: "boolean",
    },
    enable: {
      description:
        "Condition on another value that enables/disables this field.",
      type: "Condition",
    },
    expand: {
      description:
        "Extra categories/fields revealed based on this field's value.",
      type: "Expand",
    },
  }}
/>

## Field kinds

| Kind                             | Renders as                                             |
| -------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------ |
| `TEXT` / `STRING`                | Single-line text input.                                |
| `TEXTAREA`                       | Multi-line text.                                       |
| `NUMBER`                         | Numeric input with optional `min`/`max`/`step`/`unit`. |
| `SLIDER`                         | Range slider.                                          |
| `MEM`                            | Memory picker (e.g. for JVM heap / container memory).  |
| `BOOLEAN`                        | Toggle.                                                |
| `SELECT`                         | Dropdown over `options`.                               |
| `COMBOBOX`                       | Searchable dropdown over `options`.                    |
| `LIST`                           | Editable list of values.                               |
| `PASSWORD`                       | Masked input.                                          |
| `DATE` / `DATETIME` / `TIMEZONE` | Date, date-time and timezone pickers.                  |
| `READ_ONLY`                      | Displayed but not editable.                            |

## Conditions

`enable` gates a field on another value. A condition is one of:

```json
{ "equals": "PAPER" }
{ "notEquals": false }
{ "in": ["FABRIC", "QUILT"] }
```

## Expanding sections

`expand` reveals additional fields or whole categories when a value matches. Use it to keep the
form small until a choice makes more options relevant:

```json
{
  "key": "TYPE",
  "label": "Server type",
  "description": "Which server software to run.",
  "kind": "SELECT",
  "options": [
    { "value": "VANILLA", "label": "Vanilla" },
    {
      "value": "FORGE",
      "label": "Forge",
      "expand": {
        "children": [
          {
            "key": "FORGE_VERSION",
            "label": "Forge version",
            "description": "Leave empty for the recommended build.",
            "kind": "TEXT"
          }
        ]
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

Expansion can also hang off the field itself with a `when` condition:

```json
"expand": {
  "when": { "equals": true },
  "children": [ /* shown only when the toggle is on */ ]
}
```

The official Minecraft template combines these heavily: selecting a mod platform expands its
version fields, enabling the whitelist expands the player list, and so on. It's the best worked
example of a large, humane settings form.
